response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems engineer with broad multi-language exposure, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. His C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no demonstrable experience with SQL Server or Oracle, no Fixed Income or capital-markets domain knowledge, and his primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with web and distributed systems focus. RIDE Capital represents the closest financial-domain exposure, but it covers fintech investment tooling rather than capital markets or fixed-income instrumentation.
strengths: Strong CI/CD, DevOps, and developer-tooling background demonstrates solid engineering discipline
Broad multi-language background (Java, DLang, C#, Node.js) shows adaptability across runtime environments
Demonstrated communication ability through conference talks, workshops, and open-source community engagement
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months professional) and shows no Windows-platform focus — disqualifying against the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database work is MongoDB and MySQL in web contexts
Zero Fixed Income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure
